items.title: Intelligent Status Synchronization

items.implementationPlan:
# Intelligent Status Synchronization Plan

## Objective
Implement automatic status propagation up the hierarchy chain (Task -> Story -> Epic).

## Rules
- **Activation**: If a child moves to `IN_PROGRESS`, propagate `IN_PROGRESS` to the parent.
- **Completion**: If ALL children of a parent are `DONE`, move the parent to `DONE`.
- **Recursion**: Apply these rules recursively up to the top-level items.

## Implementation Steps
1. Add a recursive `syncParentStatus` helper in the API server.
2. Hook into the `updateItem` logic.
3. Ensure WebSocket broadcasts are triggered for every automated change.

items.implementationPlan:
# Mandatory Verification Workflow Plan

## Objective
Enforce code quality by requiring automated checks before a task can transition from `IN_PROGRESS` to `DONE`.

## Workflow Rules
1. **Change Detection**: The agent must detect if changes were made to `packages/server`, `packages/cli`, `packages/storage-json`, or `packages/ui`.
2. **Mandatory Check**: 
   - Backend: `npm run build` in root or specific backend package.
   - Frontend: `npm run build --workspace packages/ui`.
3. **Status Transition**:
   - Instead of moving to `DONE`, items with code changes MUST move to `REVIEW` first.
   - The agent (or a CI/CD process) performs the check.
   - SUCCESS -> Move to `DONE`.
   - FAILURE -> Move back to `IN_PROGRESS` and report errors.

## Implementation Steps
1. Update `SKILL.md` with the new protocol.
2. Add a `verify_changes` tool to the MCP server that executes the appropriate build/lint commands based on changed files.
3. Update `workflow_gatekeeper` or add a new `status_guard` to enforce the `REVIEW` step.

# Framework Global Rename Plan

## Objective
Update all branding and technical references to use 'AgenFK' instead of 'AgenFK'.

## Target Mappings
- `agenfk` -> `agenfk`
- `AgenFK` -> `AgenFK`
- `AGENFK` -> `AGENFK`

## Implementation Steps
1. Search and replace in all source files (`packages/*`, `root`).
2. Update package names in `package.json` files.
3. Update configuration file names and directory names (e.g., `.agenfk` -> `.agenfk`).
4. Update environment variables.
5. Update Opencode MCP configuration.
6. Verify with a full build.

items.title: Archival System
items.description: Implement an 'Archived' status with recursive behavior, default exclusion from lists, and UI support for collapsed viewing and bulk archival.
items.implementationPlan:
# Archival System Implementation Plan

## Objective
Add a robust archival system to AgenFK to declutter the workspace while preserving data and relationships.

## Requirements
- New `ARCHIVED` status.
- Items track their `previousStatus`.
- Recursive archival: archiving a parent archives all descendants.
- Recursive restoration: unarchiving restores the original state tree.
- UI: Collapsed column at the end of the board.
- UI: Bulk archive for columns.
- API/MCP: Exclude archived items by default.

## Tasks
1. **Core & Storage**: Add `ARCHIVED` status and `previousStatus` field.
2. **API Logic**: Implement recursive archival/unarchival logic.
3. **API Logic**: Filter archived items from default `list_items` query.
4. **UI**: Implement the collapsed "Archived" column.
5. **UI**: Add archive/unarchive actions to cards and headers.

items.title: Dashboard shows "Loading..." for Current Project after agenfk up
items.description:
After running `agenfk up`, the dashboard header shows the current project name as "Loading..." indefinitely.

Root cause: `selectedProjectId` is initialized from `localStorage.getItem('agenfk_project_id')` (KanbanBoard.tsx:39). After projects load, `activeProject = projects?.find(p => p.id === selectedProjectId)` returns `undefined` if the stored ID doesn't match any project in the current DB (e.g. after a DB reset or fresh start). Since `selectedProjectId` is non-null, the project picker screen is skipped, and the header falls back to the "Loading..." string.

Fix: Add a `useEffect` that detects when projects are loaded but `selectedProjectId` has no match — then either auto-select the project (if only one exists) or reset `selectedProjectId` to show the picker.

items.title: agenfk up doesn't kill existing server before starting, causing port 3000 conflicts with system install
items.description:
When `agenfk up` runs, it executes `start-services.sh` without first ensuring port 3000 is free. If a stale server (especially from `~/.agenfk-system`) is already listening on 3000, the repo's server either fails silently or races to the port. The system install server serves an empty database, making all projects disappear from the UI.

Fix: Before spawning services in `start-services.sh`, add a port 3000 check and kill any existing process on that port. This ensures a clean start regardless of prior state.

items.title: Implement cycle time in Web UI header and cards
items.description: Implement cycle time display in the Web UI. Show total/average cycle time in the dashboard header and individual cycle time/age on each kanban card. Format as 'Xd Yh'.

items.title: Achieve 80% Code Coverage with Vitest
items.description: Improve code quality and reliability by ensuring at least 80% code coverage across all packages in the AgenFK framework. This involves setting up Vitest as the testing framework and implementing unit/integration tests for each component.
items.implementationPlan:
# Implementation Plan: 80% Code Coverage with Vitest

## Phase 1: Setup
1.  **Install Vitest & Coverage Tools**: Install `vitest` and `@vitest/coverage-v8` in the monorepo root.
2.  **Configure Vitest**: Create a `vitest.config.ts` or `vitest.workspace.ts` to manage all packages.
3.  **Add Test Scripts**: Update the root and individual `package.json` files with `test` and `test:coverage` scripts.

## Phase 2: Unit Testing Core & Storage
1.  **@agenfk/core**: Implement unit tests for all core types and utility functions.
2.  **@agenfk/storage-json**: Implement integration tests for JSON persistence, ensuring atomic operations.

## Phase 3: Testing Server & CLI
1.  **@agenfk/server**: Implement integration tests for the REST API and WebSocket events.
2.  **@agenfk/cli**: Implement unit tests for commands and input handlers, mocking user interaction.

## Phase 4: UI Testing
1.  **ui**: Set up `jsdom` and `@testing-library/react` to test UI components and hooks.

## Phase 5: Verification & Cleanup
1.  **Continuous Coverage**: Verify all packages hit the 80% mark.
2.  **Linting & Style**: Ensure all test files follow project conventions.

items.title: Bug: Gatekeeper fails due to parent status propagation
items.description: The `workflow_gatekeeper` tool in the MCP server currently counts all items with `status: "IN_PROGRESS"`. However, the API server automatically moves parent items (Stories, Epics) to `IN_PROGRESS` whenever a child item is started. This leads to the gatekeeper reporting "AMBIGUOUS WORKFLOW" and blocking work even when only one leaf task is actually being worked on. Fix: Gatekeeper should only count leaf items or filter by type (TASK/BUG).

items.title: Automated 'TEST' Column and Coverage Enforcement
items.description: Implement a new 'TEST' status and Kanban column. This stage will automate the verification process by running test suites and enforcing strict coverage requirements (80% for new code and maintaining project-wide coverage) before an item can progress to Review or Done.
items.implementationPlan:
# Implementation Plan: Automated 'TEST' Column

## Phase 1: Core Schema & Types
1.  **Update Status Enum**: Add `TEST` status to `packages/core/src/types.ts`.
2.  **Migration**: Ensure existing logic handles the new status (e.g. status propagation, gatekeeper).

## Phase 2: UI Implementation
1.  **Update KanbanBoard**: Add the 'TEST' column between 'IN_PROGRESS' and 'REVIEW'.
2.  **Visuals**: Add specific border color and icons for the TEST column.
3.  **Real-time Updates**: Ensure the column refreshes correctly.

## Phase 3: Automation Logic (Server)
1.  **Status Transition Hook**: Modify the server to trigger a "Verification Command" when an item moves to `TEST`.
2.  **Enforce Coverage**: Implement a script that calculates coverage for *only* the changed lines (using git diff) and verifies it meets the 80% threshold.
3.  **Automated Success**: If tests and coverage pass, automatically move the item to `REVIEW`. If fail, move back to `IN_PROGRESS` or stay in `TEST` with a failed badge.

## Phase 4: Integration
1.  **Verify Tool Update**: Update `verify_changes` MCP tool to optionally support the TEST flow.
2.  **Dogfooding**: Move this epic's tasks through the new column.

items.title: Rename slash command to agenfk-release
items.description: Rename all remaining occurrences of 'agenfk-push' to 'agenfk-release', including command files, install instructions, and documentation.

reviews.command: test -f commands/agenfk-release.md && ! test -f commands/agenfk-push.md && grep -q "/agenfk-release" install.sh && grep -q "/agenfk-release" README.md

items.title: Fix hardcoded CURRENT_VERSION in CLI and sync versions
items.description: Update the CURRENT_VERSION in the CLI to correctly read from package.json and ensure all packages are updated to the latest version (0.1.5), fixing the bug where 'upgrade' always performs an upgrade.

reviews.command: cd /home/danielp/agefk/agentic-framework && npm run build -w packages/cli && node packages/cli/bin/agenfk.js --version && node packages/cli/bin/agenfk.js upgrade

items.title: Sync versions in /agenfk-release command
items.description: Update the /agenfk-release command to automatically sync version numbers across all package.json files before creating a GitHub release.

reviews.command: grep -q "Sync Version" commands/agenfk-release.md && grep -q "chore: bump version" commands/agenfk-release.md

items.title: Enforce Conventional Commits in AgenFK
items.description: Update SKILL.md and release commands to enforce Conventional Commits (fix:, feat:, etc.) instead of custom prefixes like 'close' or 'update'.

reviews.command: grep -q "Conventional Commits" SKILL.md && grep -q "fix:" SKILL.md && grep -q "feat:" SKILL.md

items.title: Add 'restart' command and auto-restart on upgrade

items.title: Enforce actual tests for verification in workflow
items.description: Update the framework's workflow and instructions to prioritize actual tests over simple builds for verification. Ensure stack detection is used to determine the correct test command.

reviews.command: grep -q "Mandatory Tests" SKILL.md && grep -q "primary testing solution" SKILL.md && grep -q "not just builds" AFK_ARCHITECTURE.md

items.title: Improve CLI version detection and upgrade logic
items.description: Improve version detection in CLI to avoid false 'up to date' messages. Use semver comparison and ensure package.json is read reliably. Fix the hardcoded fallback which is outdated.

reviews.command: cd /home/danielp/agefk/agentic-framework && npm run build -w packages/cli && node packages/cli/bin/agenfk.js upgrade | grep -q "Local version:" && node packages/cli/bin/agenfk.js upgrade | grep -q "Remote version:" && node packages/cli/bin/agenfk.js upgrade --help | grep -q "\-\-force"
reviews.output:
> @agenfk/cli@0.1.6 build
> tsc

items.title: Implement item commenting system
items.description: Implement a commenting system in AgenFK to allow agents and users to log progress steps directly on items. Includes core type updates, UI integration, and a new MCP tool.

items.title: Update core types for comments
items.description: Add CommentRecord interface and comments field to BaseItem in core types.

items.title: UI integration for comments in Overview tab
items.description: Integrate the commenting system into the CardDetailModal UI. Display comments in a 'Progress Log' section at the bottom of the 'Overview' tab.

items.title: Verify add_comment tool functionality
items.description: Final verification of the commenting system by logging a step via the new add_comment tool.

comments.content: Verified the commenting system is working correctly. Comments appear at the bottom of the Overview tab.
comments.author: opencode

items.title: UI: Align Metrics and Hierarchy card heights
items.description: Align the height of Metrics and Hierarchy cards in CardDetailModal by using flexbox stretching.

comments.content: Applying flexbox stretching to Metrics and Hierarchy cards to ensure equal height. Unifying icon container sizing for visual consistency.
comments.author: opencode

items.title: Add --beta flag to upgrade command
items.description: Add a --beta flag to the upgrade command to allow installing pre-release versions of the framework. Update the fetch logic to use 'gh release list' when beta is requested.

comments.content: Starting implementation of --beta flag for the upgrade command. Plan: 1) Update commander options, 2) Conditionalize gh CLI command to fetch pre-releases if requested.
comments.author: agent

comments.content: Updated CLI source code. Added --beta flag and logic to toggle between 'gh release view' (stable) and 'gh release list' (all). Starting verification build.
comments.author: agent

comments.content: Implementation complete and verified. Added --beta flag to 'upgrade' command. Verified that it correctly interacts with GitHub CLI to fetch the latest release (including pre-releases when requested). All CLI unit tests passed.
comments.author: agent

reviews.command: cd /home/danielp/agefk/agentic-framework && npm run build -w packages/cli && node packages/cli/bin/agenfk.js upgrade --help | grep -q "\-\-beta" && node packages/cli/bin/agenfk.js upgrade --beta | grep -q "including betas"
reviews.output:
> @agenfk/cli@0.1.8 build
> tsc

items.title: Create /agenfk-release-beta slash command
items.description: Create a new slash command /agenfk-release-beta to handle pre-releases. This command will automate version bumping with beta suffixes and create GitHub pre-releases.

comments.content: Starting creation of /agenfk-release-beta slash command. This will include logic for handling pre-release tags and the --prerelease flag in GitHub CLI.
comments.author: agent

reviews.command: test -f commands/agenfk-release-beta.md && grep -q "/agenfk-release-beta" install.sh && grep -q "/agenfk-release-beta" README.md

items.title: Implement 'Plan & Pause' workflow for complex requests
items.description: Update the framework workflow to handle complex requests by creating sub-items and pausing for user review before implementation begins.

comments.content: Starting implementation of 'Plan & Pause' workflow. I will update the global skills to mandate that complex requests (Epics/Stories) require sub-item creation and a pause for user review before any implementation begins.
comments.author: agent

comments.content: Updating SKILL.md to include the 'Plan & Pause' protocol. This will force the agent to decompose complex tasks and wait for human confirmation before starting work.
comments.author: agent

reviews.command: grep -q "Complexity & Decomposition" SKILL.md && grep -q "PAUSE" SKILL.md && grep -q "Planning Phase" AFK_ARCHITECTURE.md

items.title: Multi-Agent Workflow Orchestration
items.description: Implement a multi-agent orchestration layer within the AgenFK framework to spawn specialized agents for planning, coding, reviewing, testing, and closing units of work.

items.title: Architectural Design for Multi-Agent Orchestration
items.description: Define how specialized agents are triggered, how they share context, and how they hand off work between phases.

items.title: Phase 1: Planning Agent (Decomposition)
items.description: Implement specialized agent for task decomposition. Agent must scan codebase and existing items to suggest meaningful sub-tasks.

comments.content: Creating the /agenfk-plan slash command. This command will be used by Planning Agents to scan the codebase, understand the scope of an EPIC/STORY, and propose a detailed list of sub-tasks.
comments.author: agent

reviews.command: test -f commands/agenfk-plan.md && grep -q "Planning Agent" commands/agenfk-plan.md && grep -q "PAUSE" commands/agenfk-plan.md

items.title: Phase 3: Review Agent (Security & Requirements)
items.description: Implement specialized agent for code review. Agent must check for security vulnerabilities, architectural consistency, and adherence to requirements.

comments.content: Creating the /agenfk-review slash command. Review Agents will use this to perform security audits, verify requirement coverage, and ensure the implementation follows the system's architecture.
comments.author: agent

reviews.command: test -f commands/agenfk-review.md && grep -q "Review Agent" commands/agenfk-review.md && grep -q "Security Audit" commands/agenfk-review.md

items.title: Phase 4: Testing Agent (Coverage & Regressions)
items.description: Implement specialized agent for testing. Agent must generate missing unit/integration tests and ensure 80% coverage is met.

comments.content: Creating the /agenfk-test slash command. Testing Agents will use this to generate unit tests, execute them, and verify that the 80% coverage mandate is fulfilled.
comments.author: agent

reviews.command: test -f commands/agenfk-test.md && grep -q "Testing Agent" commands/agenfk-test.md && grep -q "80% threshold" commands/agenfk-test.md

items.title: Phase 5: Closing Agent (Summary & Finalization)
items.description: Implement specialized agent for closing tasks. Agent must summarize all implementation steps and log a final 'Done' comment.

comments.content: Creating the /agenfk-close slash command. Closing Agents will use this to summarize implementation details, token usage, and test outcomes into a final closing comment on the card.
comments.author: agent

reviews.command: test -f commands/agenfk-close.md && grep -q "Closing Agent" commands/agenfk-close.md && grep -q "FINAL SUMMARY" commands/agenfk-close.md

items.title: Design handover protocols and documenting architecture
items.description: Document the multi-agent handover protocols and task-spawning logic in SKILL.md and AFK_ARCHITECTURE.md.

comments.content: Updating AFK_ARCHITECTURE.md to define the Multi-Agent Orchestration layer. This section will describe how agents hand off work between phases (Planning -> Coding -> Review -> Test -> Done).
comments.author: agent

comments.content: Updating SKILL.md to include the 'Agent Spawning' protocol. This section will guide agents on using the 'task' tool to delegate phase-specific work (e.g., spawning a 'Review Agent' during the Review phase).
comments.author: agent

reviews.command: grep -q "Multi-Agent Orchestration" AFK_ARCHITECTURE.md && grep -q "Agent Spawning" SKILL.md && grep -q "Review Agent" SKILL.md

items.title: Phase 2: Coding Agent (Implementation)
items.description: Implement specialized agent instructions for the development phase. Agent must execute the plan, write code, and log implementation steps.

comments.content: Creating the /agenfk-code slash command. Coding Agents will use this to execute implementation plans, write high-quality code, and log their progress steps.
comments.author: agent

reviews.command: test -f commands/agenfk-code.md && grep -q "Coding Agent" commands/agenfk-code.md && grep -q "implementationPlan" commands/agenfk-code.md

items.title: Automated Multi-Agent Handoff Protocol
items.description: Refine the orchestration protocol so that the primary agent automatically spawns specialized sub-agents using the 'task' tool during phase transitions, without requiring explicit user commands for every step.

items.title: Automate phase handoff in SKILL.md
items.description: Update SKILL.md to mandate automated phase handoff using the 'task' tool.

reviews.command: grep -q "Automated Lifecycle Orchestration" SKILL.md && grep -q "MUST act as proactive orchestrators" SKILL.md && grep -q "supervisor" AFK_ARCHITECTURE.md

items.title: UI: Persist selection and navigation on updates
items.description: Fix UI bug where navigation path resets to 'Top Level' on every item update. Ensure selected item remains synchronized with fresh data from the server.

comments.content: Identifying the root cause: the server emits 'project_switched' on every item update, and the UI unconditionally resets the navigation path. I will update the UI to only reset if the project ID actually changes and add a synchronization effect for the selected item.
comments.author: agent

comments.content: Implementation complete. The navigation path now only resets if the project ID actually changes. Added a synchronization effect to ensure the selected item modal reflects real-time updates without losing selection. Build and existing tests passed.
comments.author: agent

comments.content:
Implemented Copy ID functionality for Kanban cards:
- Added Copy and Check icons from lucide-react.
- Added copiedId state and handleCopyId function in KanbanBoard.tsx.
- Added hover effect and tooltip for the ID element.
- Verified that the UI build passes.

Note: Unit tests for the UI package are failing project-wide due to a 'React.act is not a function' error in the vitest environment with React 19. This is not related to the changes made in this task as it persists even when reverting changes and for other components.
The changes have been verified via build and code review.
comments.author: agent

items.title: UI: Add Copy ID to Card Detail ModalHeader
items.description: Add a "Copy ID" button next to the item ID in the modal header.

comments.content: Modified `verify_changes` tool in `agentic-framework/packages/server/src/index.ts` to keep items in `REVIEW` status upon success instead of moving to `TEST`. This aligns the implementation with the documented Review-Test workflow. Verified the change by building the server and running existing tests.

items.title: Clarify Review-to-Test handover in skills
items.description: Update skill instructions to explicitly state that the Review Agent is responsible for the transition from REVIEW to TEST.

items.title: Enforce Agent Atomicity and Handover Protocol
items.description: Enforce strict atomicity in specialized agents. Coding agents must stop after verification, Review agents after audit, and Test agents after coverage. Handover is orchestrated exclusively by the Supervisor.

reviews.output:
---
description: Generate tests and verify coverage requirements
---

You are executing the `/agenfk-test <id>` command as a **Testing Agent**. Follow these steps precisely:

**Step 1 — Identify Test Surface**
- Read the item details using `get_item(id)`.
- Use `git diff` or compare against the parent branch to see the files modified.
- Locate the corresponding test files (e.g., `*.test.ts`, `test_*.py`).

**Step 2 — Generate Missing Tests**
- If new logic was added without tests, generate the necessary test cases using the project's testing framework.
- Ensure edge cases and error paths are covered.

**Step 3 — Execute & Verify Coverage**
- Run the project's test suite with coverage reporting (e.g., `npm run test:coverage`).
- Read the coverage report and identify any files modified in this task that fall below the **80% threshold**.
- If coverage is too low, add more tests until the threshold is met.

**Step 4 — Verify & Yield**
- If tests pass and coverage is met, use `verify_changes(id, "<test-command>")` to transition the task to REVIEW.
- DO NOT transition to DONE.
- STOP and YIELD immediately after verification. The supervisor will assign a closing agent to finalize the task.
- If failed, use `add_comment(id, "TESTS FAILED: ... [65% Coverage]")`, call `update_item(id, {status: "IN_PROGRESS"})`, and log the coverage gaps.

- If new logic was added without tests, generate the necessary test cases using the project's testing framework.
- Ensure edge cases and error paths are covered.

**Step 3 — Execute & Verify Coverage**
- Run the project's test suite with coverage reporting (e.g., `npm run test:coverage`).
- Read the coverage report and identify any files modified in this task that fall below the **80% threshold**.
- If coverage is too low, add more tests until the threshold is met.

**Step 4 — Verify & Yield**
- If tests pass and coverage is met, use `verify_changes(id, "<test-command>")` to transition the task to REVIEW.
- DO NOT transition to DONE.
- STOP and YIELD immediately after verification. The supervisor will assign a closing agent to finalize the task.
- If failed, use `add_comment(id, "TESTS FAILED: ... [65% Coverage]")`, call `update_item(id, {status: "IN_PROGRESS"})`, and log the coverage gaps.

---
name: agenfk
description: Agile, measurable, and reliable workflow enforcement for AI-assisted engineering.
compatibility: opencode
metadata:
  framework: agenfk
  workflow: agile
---

# AgenFK Engineering Framework (agenfk)

This skill enforces the core AgenFK Engineering workflow to ensure all software tasks are Agile, Measurable, Visual, Repeatable, Reliable, and Flexible.

## What I do

1.  **Initialization**
    *   **Action**:
        1. Check for `.agenfk/project.json` in the project root.
        2. If missing, call `list_projects()` via MCP to see existing projects.
        3. Ask the user if they want to use an existing project or create a new one (recommended).
        4. Create/link the project by creating `.agenfk/project.json` with the `{ "projectId": "..." }`.
        5. Scan the codebase and generate `AFK_PROJECT_SCOPE.md` and `AFK_ARCHITECTURE.md` if they don't exist. BE THOROUGH AND COMPLETE. If generating these, reason about the codebase and ask clarifying questions using the environment's Question UI to confirm architectural decisions before writing the files.
        6. Fetch all items via `list_items(projectId)` and render the **Board Report** as described below.
    *   **Board Report Format**:
        *   **Cycle Time Calculation**: For each item, compute cycle time as `updatedAt - createdAt`. Format durations as `HH:MM:SS`. For DONE/ARCHIVED items this represents total elapsed time. For active items (TODO, IN_PROGRESS, BLOCKED) it represents age.
        *   **Per-card display**: Include a `Cycle Time` column in every status group table.
        *   **Summary Header**: Show a metrics line with:
            - Total token usage (sum of all `tokenUsage[].input + tokenUsage[].output` across all items)
            - Total cycle time (sum of cycle times of all DONE items, formatted as `HH:MM:SS`)
            - Average cycle time across DONE items (total ÷ count, formatted as `HH:MM:SS`)
            - Format: `Tokens: X in / Y out | Cycle Total: HH:MM:SS | Cycle Avg: HH:MM:SS (N tasks)`
    *   **Objective**: Maintain project identity and a living map.

2.  **Request Analysis & Clarification**
    *   **Context**: Take int account `AFK_PROJECT_SCOPE.md` and `AFK_ARCHITECTURE.md` for informed reasoning.
    *   **Action**: Call `analyze_request(request: string)` for every new user requirement.
    *   **Complexity & Decomposition**: 
        1. Evaluate if the request requires multiple steps or touches multiple components.
        2. If complex, categorize as **EPIC** or **STORY**.
        3. **Sub-item Creation**: For every EPIC/STORY, you MUST define and create the necessary sub-items (Stories for Epics, Tasks for Stories) in `TODO` status immediately after creating the parent.
        4. **Plan & Pause**: After creating the hierarchy (Parent + Children), you **MUST PAUSE** and present the proposed items to the user. Ask: "I've decomposed your request into the following items. Should I proceed with implementation or would you like to make changes?"
        5. **Authorization**: NEVER move a task to `IN_PROGRESS` until the user has confirmed the plan.
    *   **Reasoning Step**: Before creating ANY item or initializing a project, you MUST reason about the implementation details. 
    *   **Question UI**: If there are *any* ambiguities, missing technical details, or decisions to be made, you MUST use the environment's native "Question UI" (e.g., `default_api:question` in Opencode, or equivalent in other environments) to ask the user for clarification before proceeding with creation.
    *   **Objective**: Categorize as **EPIC**, **STORY**, **TASK**, or **BUG**.
    *   **Requirement**: All items created must be associated with the active `projectId`.
    *   **Transparency**: If you're opencode, display every MCP call parameter and return value.
    *   **Conventional Commits**: Use standard prefixes for all commits: `fix:`, `feat:`, `chore:`, `docs:`, `style:`, `refactor:`, `perf:`, `test:`. NEVER use `close:`, `update:`, or other non-standard prefixes. Append the item ID in brackets if relevant, e.g., `fix: resolve crash [id]`.

3.  **Planning (Epics only)**
    *   **Action**: Require or generate a detailed Markdown **Implementation Plan**.
    *   **Objective**: Ensure clear roadmaps for large features. Save in the `implementationPlan` field.

4.  **Action Authorization (Gatekeeper)**
    *   **Action**: Call `workflow_gatekeeper(intent: string, role: string)` BEFORE any code change.
    *   **Mandatory Roles**: You MUST specify your current role: `planning`, `coding`, `review`, `testing`, or `closing`.
    *   **Mechanical Enforcement**: The gatekeeper will reject authorization if your `role` does not match the status of the active task (e.g., `role="coding"` requires status `IN_PROGRESS`).
    *   **CRITICAL**: Always use MCP tools (`create_item`, `update_item`, `verify_changes`, `log_token_usage`) for ALL workflow state changes. **Never use the `agenfk` CLI to create items, update status, or close tasks.** The CLI bypasses the enforcement layer built into the MCP server.

5.  **Mandatory Automated Testing (Agent Driven)**
    *   **Action**: Moving an item to the `TEST` column (status: `TEST`) is a signal that the Agent (Opencode/Claude) must now perform deep verification.
    *   **Requirement**: The Agent MUST run the project's test suite (e.g., `npm run test:coverage`) using its local tools.
    *   **Coverage Rule**: New code MUST be covered at 80% minimum.
    *   **Workflow**: 
        *   The `verify_changes` tool automatically moves items from `REVIEW` to `TEST` upon success.
        *   The Agent verifies coverage and regressions in `TEST`.
        *   Success: Agent moves item to `DONE`.
        *   Failure: Agent moves item back to `IN_PROGRESS`.

6.  **Final Verification (Review Tool)**
    *   **Action**: BEFORE moving to `TEST`, the Agent **MUST** use the `verify_changes(itemId, command)` tool.
    *   **Mandatory Tests**: The `command` passed to `verify_changes` MUST include the project's primary testing solution (e.g., `npm test`, `pytest`, `go test`). Simple builds (`npm run build`) are NOT sufficient. The Agent must perform stack detection (e.g., checking `package.json`, `requirements.txt`, `go.mod`) to identify the correct test command.
    *   **Transition Logic (Automated by Tool)**:
        1. The tool moves the item to `REVIEW`.
        2. The tool executes the command.
        3. Success: Moves to `TEST`. Failure: Moves back to `IN_PROGRESS`.

7.  **Measurement & Tracking**
    *   **Reporting Requirements**: The Agent **MUST** call `log_token_usage(itemId, input, output, model)` immediately after marking an item as `DONE` (e.g., following a successful `verify_changes`), or at the end of a significant session of work for an `IN_PROGRESS` item.
    *   **Progress Comments**: The Agent **MUST** call `add_comment(itemId, content)` for EVERY significant step performed during implementation (e.g. "Modified core types", "Updated UI components", "Ran tests"). This ensures the human user can follow the agent's work in real-time on the Kanban board.
    *   **Estimation**: If exact token counts are not available in the environment, the Agent **MUST** provide a reasonable estimate. **Do not skip this step.**
    *   **Completion**: Update parent Story/Epic status automatically.

## Automated Lifecycle Orchestration
AgenFK agents MUST act as proactive orchestrators of the work lifecycle. You are responsible for automatically spawning specialized sub-agents via the `task` tool at every phase transition. DO NOT wait for explicit user instructions to proceed between phases once the initial plan is approved.

*   **Plan & Pause**: For complex requests (EPIC/STORY), you MUST decompose the task, create all sub-items, and PAUSE for human approval. Once approved, the rest of the lifecycle is autonomous.
*   **Automatic Handover (Coding to Review)**: Immediately after a successful implementation and `verify_changes` call (moving item to `REVIEW`), you MUST spawn a "Review Agent" via `task(subagent_type="explore", prompt="/agenfk-review <id>")`.
*   **Automatic Handover (Review to Test)**: If the Review Agent returns success, move the item to `TEST` and immediately spawn a "Test Agent" via `task(subagent_type="general", prompt="/agenfk-test <id>")`.
*   **Automatic Handover (Test to Done)**: If the Test Agent returns success, move the item to `DONE` and spawn a "Closing Agent" via `task(subagent_type="general", prompt="/agenfk-close <id>")` to summarize and log the final comment.

If any sub-agent fails, move the item back to `IN_PROGRESS` and report the specific errors to the user.

## When to use me

Use this skill whenever you are performing software engineering tasks to ensure compliance with the AgenFK Engineering Framework.

## Available Tools (MCP: agenfk)

*   `list_projects`: List all existing projects.
*   `create_project`: Create a new project.
*   `create_item`: Create a new workflow item (requires `projectId`).
*   `update_item`: Update status or properties.
*   `list_items`: Query the backlog (filter by `projectId`).
*   `get_item`: Get full details.
*   `log_token_usage`: Record resource consumption.
*   `add_context`: Attach relevant file paths.
*   `analyze_request`: Categorization strategy.
*   `workflow_gatekeeper`: Pre-flight authorization and role verification.
*   `verify_changes`: Execute dynamic syntax/build checks and update status.
*   `get_server_info`: Framework health check.

5. Phase Commands (Agent Spawn): '/agenfk-plan', '/agenfk-code', '/agenfk-review', '/agenfk-test', '/agenfk-close'.
6. Run 'agenfk health' to verify your installation at any time.

comments.content:
Addressed all Review Agent findings for cross-platform compatibility:
1. Replaced Unix-specific commands (fuser, pkill, rm -rf) with Node.js alternatives in CLI and scripts.
2. Replaced process.env.HOME with os.homedir() (verified and rebuilt).
3. Fixed hardcoded absolute paths in support scripts by using rootDir-relative paths.
4. Updated CLI and installers to invoke .mjs scripts instead of .sh.
5. Ensured consistency between install.mjs and start-services.mjs.
6. Replaced .sh scripts with bash wrappers around Node.js implementations.
All 65 source tests passed.
comments.author: agent

comments.content:
### Shell-to-Node Migration Summary

1. **Logic Migration:** Successfully migrated all installation logic from `install.sh` to `scripts/install.mjs`.
2. **Modernization:** Utilized ES Modules, `fs.promises`, and robust child process handling for building packages and configuring the environment.
3. **Cross-Platform:** The new script ensures compatibility across different OS environments by avoiding shell-specific syntax.
4. **Features Reimplemented:**
   - Workspace builds (`npm run build -w ...`)
   - Secret token generation (`~/.agenfk/verify-token`)
   - Background service script creation (`start-services.mjs`)
   - MCP configuration for Opencode and Claude Code.
   - Skill and command installation to standard paths.
   - Integration with `CLAUDE.md` and `settings.json`.
5. **Verification:** Validated via `node scripts/install.mjs --help` and full execution logs.

Migration is complete and stable.

items.title: Task: Migrate start-services.sh to start-services.js
items.description: Migrate start-services.sh logic to a Node.js script.

items.title: Migrate install.sh to scripts/install.mjs
items.description:
Migrate all logic from install.sh to a cross-platform Node.js script at scripts/install.mjs.
Ensure it works on Windows, macOS, and Linux.
Use 'fs/promises', 'os', 'path', and 'child_process'.

items.title: Address Review Agent findings for cross-platform compatibility
items.description:
Address all findings from the Review Agent:
1. Replace Unix-specific commands (fuser, pkill, rm -rf) with Node.js alternatives.
2. Replace process.env.HOME with os.homedir().
3. Fix hardcoded absolute paths in support scripts.
4. Update CLI to invoke .mjs scripts instead of .sh.
5. Ensure consistency between install.mjs and start-services.mjs.
